# Figure description: Gamma VII X and XI.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/geophysical sections/Gamma VII X and XI.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geophysical cross-section figure** containing multiple **gravity and/or magnetic anomaly profiles** combined with interpreted geological cross-sections beneath each profile. The figure contains three separate profile panels arranged across the sheet, each showing: (1) an upper curve representing measured geophysical anomaly values, (2) a lower curve showing a transformed or residual anomaly component, and (3) a schematic geological cross-section with density/susceptibility parameters assigned to interpreted rock bodies. This is a standard Soviet-era format for combined gravity (Δg) and/or magnetic (ΔZ or ΔT) interpretation profiles used in regional geological-geophysical surveys.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers **three geophysical survey profiles** labeled:
- **Профиль VII** (Profile VII) — left panel, the largest and most detailed
- **Профиль X** (Profile X) — central panel
- **Профиль XI** (Profile XI) — right panel

These profiles are from the regional geological-geophysical survey of northern Kazakhstan (Report No. **36572**, Smolianova et al., 1983–1984), covering map sheets **M-42 and N-42** at 1:200,000 scale. The profiles appear to traverse areas of interest for subsurface structural mapping, likely crossing zones of contrasting rock density and/or magnetic susceptibility. The horizontal scale appears to extend to approximately **1200–1500 units** (likely meters or survey point numbers) along each profile. The vertical axes show anomaly values in **мГл (milligals)** for gravity and/or **нТл or гамма** for magnetic measurements, with values ranging from approximately **-150 to +400** on Profile VII, suggesting a strong combined gravity-magnetic response. Profile X and XI show more subdued but still significant anomaly ranges.

---

## Key Content

### Profile VII (Left Panel — Most Prominent):
- The **upper anomaly curve** shows a very large, sharp **positive anomaly peak** reaching approximately **+350–400 мГл** (or corresponding magnetic units), with a sharp rise and fall — characteristic of a localized, near-surface high-density or high-susceptibility body (possibly a mafic/ultramafic intrusion or a buried crystalline basement high).
- To the left of the peak, the curve shows a broad **negative anomaly** (approximately **-100 to -150**), indicating a low-density or low-susceptibility zone (possibly sedimentary basin fill or a granite body).
- The **lower panel** (residual or second derivative curve) mirrors this pattern with a pronounced negative trough reaching approximately **-150**, suggesting a deep compensating mass deficit.
- The **geological cross-section** at the bottom of this profile shows several interpreted rock bodies labeled with **density values** (σ, in g/cm³), including values such as **σ = 2.67 г/см³**, **σ = 2.84 г/см³**, and others, delineating zones of contrasting rock types (likely basement crystalline rocks, sedimentary cover, and intrusive bodies).
- Annotated points (circled numbers: **①, ②, ③** etc.) mark interpreted geological contacts or borehole intersections projected onto the profile.
- The cross-section shows a **near-surface structural high** (possibly a horst or anticline in basement) beneath the anomaly peak, flanked by deeper basins.

### Profile X (Central Panel):
- The **upper curve** is more subdued, showing a broad, gentle **positive plateau** at approximately **+100–150 units**, with minor undulations — consistent with a broader, deeper source or a more gradual lateral density contrast.
- The **lower curve** (residual) shows a very prominent **negative trough** extending well below the zero line, reaching approximately **-400 to -500 units**, which is unusually deep and may indicate a significant mass deficit or a large sedimentary basin structure at depth.
- No geological cross-section is visible beneath Profile X in this panel, suggesting it may have been placed on a separate sheet or was omitted from this figure.

### Profile XI (Right Panel):
- The **upper curve** shows several **moderate positive anomaly peaks** (approximately **+100–150 units**), with more irregular, multi-peaked character — suggesting multiple discrete sources or a more complex structural setting.
- The **lower curve** shows a **negative trough** of moderate amplitude.
- The **geological cross-section** at the bottom is similar in format to Profile VII, with labeled density values including **σ = 2.67 г/см³**, **σ = 2.84 г/см³**, and additional parameters, with annotated contact points.
- Annotated borehole or reference points are visible (circled symbols).

### General Observations:
- All profiles use a **two-layer anomaly representation**: observed field above, and a residual/transformed component below the zero line.
- The geological cross-sections use **hachured and patterned symbols** to distinguish rock types (typical Soviet geological notation).
- Density contrast values suggest the interpreted bodies include **dense mafic/crystalline basement rocks** (σ ~2.84) versus **less dense sedimentary or granitic rocks** (σ ~2.67).
- The figure note at bottom reads: **"Условные обозначения на листе 4.1"** — meaning the legend/key is provided on sheet 4.1 of the report.

- Center panel: **Профиль X** (Profile X)
- Right panel: **Профиль XI** (Profile XI)

- Y-axis lower panels: **мГл** (milligals) or residual values

- Zero line marked on both upper and lower graphs
